channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: Atomfall TV Show in the Works from Two Brothers Pictures
item.content:encoded:
Rebellion and production company Two Brothers Pictures have announced that Atomfall is getting a TV show adaptation, and that it'll be worked on by a producer-writer duo who've worked on the likes of Fleabag and Assassin.

As reported by Eurogamer, the Atomfall TV show will be written by Harry and Jack Williams, whose previous credits include the aforementioned shows, as well as Baptiste and The Missing. Executive producer Alex Mercer, meanwhile, has worked on shows like Doctor Who and Maya.

The Williams brothers say that adapting Atomfall's "distinctive British tone and setting" for TV has been "a real joy", and that they're excited about "expanding this strange, unsettling story for television". Rebellion heads Jason and Chris Kingsley, meanwhile, say they're "delighted" by the partnership.

We don't yet know whether the Atomfall TV show will be directly adapting the story of the game or whether it'll choose to tell a more character-centric story; after all, the game is a rather freeform experience that involves organic exploration and discovery, which is a trickier proposition for a show with a linear narrative.

Either way, Atomfall being adapted for TV makes a lot of sense when considering the game's success. Having reached a million players within its first weekend (no doubt bolstered by a day one Game Pass release), the survival adventure stands as developer Rebellion's most successful launch ever.

If you haven't played Atomfall, it's a first-person survival game inspired by classic British media like The Prisoner, Doctor Who, and The Quatermass Experiment, as well as videogames like BioShock and Fallout: New Vegas.

The game sees you exploring a quaint British village and its surrounding countryside in the wake of an alternate-history version of the real-life Windscale nuclear disaster, and it lets you off the leash to explore and discover things on your own impressively early on in proceedings.

Since last year's launch, Atomfall has received two major story expansions, Wicked Isle and The Red Strain, both of which expand on some of the factions and enemies you'll encounter in the base game.

You can check out Atomfall right now on PC, PlayStation, and Xbox, and you can read our very own Erren's review of the game right here.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/atomfall-tv-show-announced
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: NACON Connect Being Held Next Week
item.content:encoded:
NACON is officially holding its Connect event in one week, as of this writing. A new press release confirms that the event will be held on May 7. This comes at a turbulent time for NACON and the video game industry as a whole. The presentation is set to focus on the company’s new projects across its accessories branch and the video games it will publish.

The event was originally scheduled for March but was delayed after the company announced it was filing for emergency insolvency. At the time, the company confidently stated that this would not be the end for them. However, the difficulties have continued to pile on. Recently, it was reported that three of NACON’s studios, Spiders, Kylotonn, and Cyanide, would also be filing for insolvency. Learn more about that at this link.

More on the Upcoming NACON Connect Event

The upcoming NACON Connect Event on May 7 is slated to feature a number of titles, including more details on the recently announced Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish. The presentation will be streamed on the company’s official YouTube and Twitch channels at 1:00 p.m. ET.

“On the program for the evening: announcements of ambitious games, a preview of upcoming innovations for its range of accessories, and exclusive gameplay sequences for anticipated titles such as The Mound, Edge of Memories, Endurance Motorsport Series, and the very recently announced Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish,” the company said in a press release.

Insider Gaming will provide full coverage of the event on May 7. For more NACON news, learn about the cancellation of several physical releases the company was planning on.

Are you excited about the upcoming event at all? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nacon-connect-being-held-next-week/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Vehicular action game Truck-kun is Supporting Me From Another World?! announced for Xbox Series, PC
item.content:encoded:
Publisher Frosty Pop and developer Strange Scaffold have announced Truck-kun is Supporting Me From Another World?!, a vehicular action game where you play as a truck that “isekais” people into another world. It will launch for Xbox Series and PC via Steam in 2026.

Here is an overview of the game, via Strange Scaffold:

About

Truck-kun is Supporting Me From Another World?! is a vehicular action game where you play as a truck that isekais people into another world.

This anime-inspired title is about smashing through objects at high speed to power up the corporate ladder climber you accidentally isekai’d into a dangerous medieval fantasy world. Inspired by the anime and manga subgenre of Isekai, in which characters find themselves in parallel universes, Truck-kun is Supporting Me From Another World?! revels in absurdism while simultaneously grounding itself with sharp satire that reflects real life.

Players start their journey with Carissa Ward, who is about to start a new job as VP of marketing in the sunny coastal town of Wenvale. That plan is cut short when she’s struck by a delivery truck and awakens in the fantasy world of Talinfold. To get home, she must unlock the Galactic Gate, defeat the Skeleton King, and claim his Scroll of Resurrection—with your help.

Good luck!!!

Key Features

  • Dodge cops, make deliveries, and smash through objects in the chaotic small town of Wenvale, USA!
  • Complete objectives across two worlds to power up a Galactic Gate with stars and unlock a subversive, heartwarming story rooted in our love for anime.
  • Run over pedestrians to turn them into monsters Carissa can slay for stars and XP. It’s fine! Don’t worry! Stop thinking about it!!
  • Energetic, ska-inspired soundtrack by award-winning composer David Mason (DREDGE, Creepy Redneck Dinosaur Mansion 3).
  • A giant, screaming man whose meat you can legally drive through.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/vehicular-action-game-truck-kun-is-supporting-me-from-another-world-announced-for-xbox-series-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Famitsu Sales: 4/20/26 – 4/26/26
item.content:encoded:
• PS5
  • Switch
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox Series

Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream remains on top.

Famitsu has published its estimated physical game software data for Japan for the week of April 20, 2026 to April 26, 2026.

Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream for Switch remained the highest selling game this week, moving another 178,533 retail copies.

The Switch 2 version of PRAGMATA also launched this week following the PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC launch the previous week, moving 14,453 units. The PlayStation 5 version also sold an additional 12,786 units.

On the hardware side, Switch 2 sold 45,825 units, the Switch family sold 27,139 units, the PlayStation 5 family sold 12,973 units, and the Xbox Series family sold 247 units.

Get the full sales charts below.

Software Sales (followed by lifetime sales)

  1. [NSW] Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream (Nintendo, 04/16/26) – 178,533 (743,938)
  2. [SW2] Pokemon Pokopia (The Pokemon Company, 03/05/26) – 17,039 (927,044)
  3. [SW2] PRAGMATA (Capcom, 04/24/26) – 14,453 (New)
  4. [PS5] PRAGMATA (Capcom, 04/17/26) – 12,786 (49,256)
  5. [NSW] Matsurika no Kei -kEi- Tenmei kashokuden (Idea Factory, 04/23/26) – 7,654 (New)
  6. [SW2] Mario Kart World (Nintendo, 06/05/25) – 5,537 (2,906,379)
  7. [SW2] Animal Crossing: New Horizons – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Nintendo, 01/15/26) – 3,695 (108,612)
  8. [NSW] Minecraft (Microsoft, 06/21/18) – 3,378 (4,193,529)
  9. [NSW] Momotaro Dentetsu 2: Anata no Machi mo Kitto Aru – Higashi Nihon Hen + Nishi Nihon Hen (Konami, 11/13/25) – 3,201 (343,741)
  10. [NSW] Elminage ORIGINAL: Priestess of Darkness and The Ring of the Gods (Mebius, 04/23/26) – 2,882 (New)

Rankings 11 to 30 will be announced on May 1.

  1. [NSW] Kingdom’s Return: Time-Eating Fruit and the Ancient Monster (Inti Creates, 04/23/26) – (New)
  2. [NSW] Animal Crossing: New Horizons (Nintendo, 03/20/20) –
  3. [NSW] Doraemon Dorayaki Shop Story (Kairosoft, 04/16/26) –
  4. [NSW] Nintendo Switch Sports (NIntendo, 04/29/22) –
  5. [NSW] Pokemon FireRed Version / LeafGreen Version (Download Card) (The Pokemon Company, 02/28/26) –
  6. [SW2] Xenoblade Chronicles X: Definitive Edition – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Nintendo, 04/16/26) –
  7. [NSW] Super Smash Bros. Ultimate (Nintendo, 12/07/18) –
  8. [PS5] Crimson Desert (Pearl Abyss, 03/19/26) –
  9. [SW2] Super Mario Bros. Wonder – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition + Meetup in Bellabel Park (Nintendo, 03/26/26) –
  10. [NSW] Mario Kart 8 Deluxe (Nintendo, 04/28/17) –
  11. [SW2] Pokemon Legends: Z-A – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (The Pokemon Company, 10/16/25) –
  12. [SW2] Resident Evil Requiem (Capcom, 02/27/26) –
  13. [NSW] Pokemon Legends: Z-A (The Pokemon Company, 10/16/25) –
  14. [SW2] Mario Tennis Fever (Nintendo, 02/12/26) –
  15. [SW2] Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection (Capcom, 03/13/26) –
  16. [NSW] Super Mario Galaxy + Super Mario Galaxy 2 (Nintendo, 10/02/25) –
  17. [SW2] Super Mario Party Jamboree – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition + Jamboree TV (Nintendo, 07/24/25) –
  18. [NSW] Super Mario Party Jamboree (Nintendo, 10/17/24) –
  19. [SW2] Momotaro Dentetsu 2: Anata no Machi mo Kitto Aru – Higashi Nihon Hen + Nishi Nihon Hen – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Konami, 11/13/25) –
  20. [PS5] Resident Evil Requiem (Capcom, 02/27/26) –
  21. [NSW] Dragon Quest VII Reimagined (Square Enix, 02/05/26) –
  22. [NSW] Splatoon 3 (Nintendo, 09/09/22) –
  23. [NSW] Clubhouse Games: 51 Worldwide Classics (Nintendo, 06/05/20) –
  24. [SW2] Kirby Air Riders (Nintendo, 11/20/25) –

Hardware Sales (followed by lifetime sales)

  1. Switch 2 – 45,825 (5,153,222)
  2. Switch Lite – 11,263 (6,942,368)
  3. Switch OLED Model – 10,796 (9,554,639)
  4. PlayStation 5 Digital Edition – 6,361 (1,266,803)
  5. Switch – 5,080 (20,284,100)
  6. PlayStation 5 Pro – 4,330 (349,972)
  7. PlayStation 5 – 2,282 (5,916,138)
  8. Xbox Series X Digital Edition – 127 (30,138)
  9. Xbox Series S – 82 (341,861)
  10. Xbox Series X – 37 (326,322)


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/famitsu-sales-4-20-26-4-26-26
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Our first trailer for Zach Cregger's Resident Evil movie has echoes of Weapons and more, but does fall a little short on lickers
item.content:encoded:
Midnight brain to gorge ya.

The first trailer for the Resident Evil movie, from Weapons director Zach Cregger, is here, and so far it doesn't seem to be all that Resident Evil.

Cregger is the latest to be tackling a Resident Evil movie adaptation, and if this trailer is anything to go by it looks very much like a horror film in the director's style, rather than a straight forward video game adaptation of Capcom's series. Though given the previous attempts, this is probably no bad thing.

Rather than being inundated with known Resident Evil mutations such as lickers or the surprise appearance of a savage dog being fended off by capable members of the STARS team, here we instead get a poor soul wearing a knitted sheep-adorned bobble hat who is clearly out of his depth.

This chap is trying to get hold of a loved one, and in his words, he is in a "seriously messed up situation right now". All in all, the trailer is quite ominous and, well, Creggery. You can see for yourself via the video below.

Cregger - whose other previous work includes 2022's Barbarian, which our Matt describes as "wonderfully unpredictable" - was first announced to be helming the next Resident Evil movie in March of last year. Since then, the director has assured fans that his adaptation will be "unlike any of the previous films" in the franchise, which seems to be so far so true.

Cregger previously said he hasn't "seen the movies" that have gone before, because "that's just not my thing". The Resident Evil game series, though, well "that's just a playground I just love," the director said last year. "I think the movie is going to rip. I think it's going to rip."

We'll find out either way in September, but at least now we have our first proper clue.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/resident-evil-movie-zach-cregger-first-trailer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fleabag Producer Two Brothers Pictures Takes on TV Show Adaptation of BAFTA-Winning Fallout-Like RPG Atomfall
item.content:encoded:
Atomfall, a very British take on games like Fallout or STALKER from Rebellion that landed on PC and consoles in March 2025, is the latest recent release to get its own adaptation. Two Brothers Pictures, the production company behind hit shows like Fleabag and The Assassin will lead up a TV show adaptation of the game, which also just took home the BAFTA for Best British Game two weeks ago.

News of the adaptation comes via a report from Deadline, who add that the Two Brothers Pictures founders, Harry and Jack Williams, will also be creative leads for the adaptation as the series main writers. It's unclear if the brothers will also handle showrunner duties, but they'll be heavily involved in the series direction regardless. Rebellion co-founder Chris Kingsley and creative director/chief executive officer Jason Kingsley will also work as executive producers on the show.

"Atomfall has such a distinctive British tone and setting, and it's been a real joy developing it alongside the Rebellion team," said Harry and Jack Williams, "especially as two brothers working alongside two brothers. There's something very exciting about expanding this strange, unsettling story for television."

The Kingsley brothers added, "Harry and Jack demonstrated a clear love for Atomfall and talked about their own particular endings when they were playing the game. It is always exciting to work with people who share the same passion for creating and telling great stories and we are sure that this partnership will help to deliver a television series that will delight fans of the game and beyond."

Atomfall's gameplay and setting similarities to a series like Fallout make it easy to see how it could work as a TV show, particularly because we've already seen how well it's worked out for the Fallout TV show, which is arguably one of the best video game to small (or big) screen adaptations we've seen in this renaissance of video game to film/TV adaptations.

It'll be interesting to see the show develop, and if Atomfall, which doesn't carry nearly the same weight in its name as its inspirations, is capable of expanding its reach as a relatively new IP by way of a TV show that could, realistically, end up being more popular than the game. Something that isn't necessarily a concern for other successful adaptations we've seen, as they're all based on established franchises.

Sure, more people might end up watching the shows/movies than play the games they're based on, but the games were established for years before the adaptations came along. We know that Atomfall has surpassed 2 million copies sold, and while Rebellion hasn't confirmed it, we could see a sequel at some point in the future.

But it would surely be something if 'Atomfall the show' became more significant than 'Atomfall the game,' and we eventually stopped seeing more games because the show kept growing.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/atomfall-tv-show-adaptation-fleabag-producers-lead/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox Boss Acknowledges Another Rough Quarter As Microsoft CEO Says The Company Needs To ‘Win Back Fans’
item.content:encoded:
New Xbox boss Asha Sharma has been working quickly to fix the broken vibes around the platform. That’s included new messaging, but also substantive changes like making Game Pass cheaper. It’s a long road ahead, though, and Microsoft’s latest earnings report showed just how much work the team has cut out for it as it tries, in the words of CEO Satya Nadella, “to win back fans.”

Xbox hardware fell 33 percent year-over-year last quarter, which is in line with previous declines for actual sales of the Xbox Series X/S. Xbox content and services revenue was also down 5 percent year-over-year, which CFO Amy Hood blamed on a stronger quarter the year prior, likely from much stronger momentum around Call of Duty Black Ops 6 than Black Ops 7. Despite every pivot and new initiative this generation, Microsoft’s gaming business is still headed in the wrong direction.

Sharma didn’t shy away from the challenge. “While we have made progress expanding the business and our margins, player and revenue growth has not yet met our ambition,” she wrote on X, now more than two months into her tenure after replacing Phil Spencer. “We know we have work to do to earn every player today and into the future.”

While Sharma touted progress on profit margins, likely due in part to cuts last summer and the steady stream of Xbox ports to PlayStation 5, Nadella touted new records “during the quarter” for monthly Xbox active users and total game streaming hours. But he also didn’t shy away from the way Microsoft’s consumer-facing side of the company, including gaming, has gone off the rails.

“When it comes to our consumer business, we’re doing the foundational work required to win back fans and strengthen engagement across Windows, Xbox, Bing, and Edge,” he said during the earnings call. He later pointed to Game Pass specifically. “You also see this in Xbox, where the team is recommitting to our core fans and players and shaping the future of play,” Nadella continued. “Last week’s Game Pass changes are one example of how we are staying responsive to customer feedback.”

It’s a clear shift from the previous tone on these calls, and markedly different from the confidence projected in the years immediately following the Activision Blizzard acquisition of a gaming platform that was on the right track. It’s still unclear what exactly the next phase of Xbox will look like, however. It could be a deep overhaul, which some fans feel is needed for a long time, or just a recalibration that achieves better KPIs.



https://kotaku.com/xbox-boss-acknowledges-another-rough-quarter-as-microsoft-ceo-says-the-company-needs-to-win-back-fans-2000692003
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Star Wars: Galactic Racer Release Date Officially Announced
item.content:encoded:
Star Wars: Galactic Racer’s release date has been officially confirmed: October 6 for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC.

Last week, an accidental leak revealed the release date for the upcoming title on a Steam page listing, along with the pre-order bonuses. Now, the official announcement has arrived, and the leaked release date and pre-order bonuses were accurate. Check out more details below:

Star Wars: Galactic Racer Release on October 6

A new post by the official Star Wars account on X (formerly Twitter) confirms the October release date for Star Wars: Galactic Racer. Interested readers can also pre-order the game from today on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC. The pre-order bonus for the game features an exclusive Livery for the player’s repulsorcraft and a Player Banner for multiplayer modes. Check more details about the game editions below:

Star Wars Galactic Racer Deluxe Edition Features

The standard edition only includes the base game, and all additional content is reserved for Deluxe Edition buyers. Players who purchase this edition will receive three exclusive vehicles: Kor Sarun: Ciza T speeder bike, the Kor Sarun: Darc X landspeeder, and the Kor Sarun: Rak S skim speeder. Players will also receive a unique livery, three Arcade events, a deluxe Player banner, and a digital artbook, which includes never-before-seen character, vehicle, and location artwork from the development.

In other news, the PowerWash Simulator 2 developers have unveiled a Star Wars DLC that arrives in Summer 2026. Additionally, Insider Gaming has learned that development on Star Wars Eclipse is “very slow going“. Will you be purchasing Star Wars Galactic Racer once it releases this October? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Rebellion’s Atomfall being turned into a TV series. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/star-wars-galactic-racer-release-date-officially-announced/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Where Is Black Ops Royale in Call of Duty Warzone 2026?
item.content:encoded:
Warzone was given a new lease of life with the release of Black Ops Royale, featuring a refreshing twist on the battle royale formula. However, you might have noticed that it’s disappeared from the Warzone playlist screen, and Activision has explained all.

Call of Duty: Warzone saw a huge resurgence (no pun intended) in 2025 with the return of Verdansk. It suddenly took everyone back in time five years, and life was good again. A similar thing happened with Black Ops Royale, a new game mode reminiscent of Black Ops 4’s Blackout.

If it’s one of the reasons you keep coming back to Warzone, you’ll want to know why it’s no longer in the menu screen.

Why Can’t I See Black Ops Royale in CoD Warzone?

Black Ops Royale was removed from the playlist options toward the end of Warzone Season 3 in 2026, meaning you can no longer see Black Ops Royale, and you can’t play the game mode moving forward.

If you’ve played any free-to-play or paid Call of Duty game before online, you’ll know that the developers like to switch up the playlist options, giving new modes a chance to flourish, while trying to make you miss other match types in the process.

  • More Insider Gaming: How Does Game Pass Price Drop Affect Call of Duty?

As good as Black Ops Royale is, the devs have decided that it won’t be available for a while.

Why Has Black Ops Royale Been Removed From Warzone?

Season 3 Reloaded’s Hot Pursuit mode is essentially replacing Black Ops Royale, and this refresh will give Hot Pursuit the spotlight for now.

As I said, if a new map or mode comes along, something has to make way for it so that it encourages you to enjoy the content the team has worked hard on. Hot Pursuit is the standout addition to Warzone Season 3 Reloaded, and here’s what the devs have said in the recent patch notes:

‘Hot Pursuit takes over the Black Ops Royale playlist for Season 03 Reloaded as a throwback to its Blackout debut back in 2019. Inspired by the original fast-paced, cat & mouse experience, this Limited-Time Mode offers a modern take on vehicle-focused gameplay now on Avalon.

During this time, core Black Ops Royale modes will be temporarily rotated out to make room for Hot Pursuit.’

There you have it; it’s an answer, but probably not the one you wanted. Now, onto the next question you presumably have!

When is Black Ops Royale Coming Back To Warzone?

Activision has said that Black Ops Royale will be ‘back in a future update,’ but it’s unclear when the game mode will return in Warzone.

  • More Insider Gaming: Black Ops 7 Director Addresses Skill-Based Damage Accusations

Here’s the full statement from the Season 3 Reloaded patch notes:

‘We plan on bringing the standard experience back in a future update, but for now, we’re keeping the playlist fresh by leaning into the spirit of variety.’

In short, Black Ops Royale will be back in maybe Season 4, or Season 4 Reloaded, but Hot Pursuit will be the focus of Warzone’s attention for quite a while.

So, will you be pursuing many matches in Warzone’s newest LTM? Or have the devs made a big mistake removing Black Ops Royale from selection? Let us know your thoughts, as they should be interesting, through our free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord.

For more CoD news, check out the incredible attack on gamers by the Call of Duty movie director, calling war games ‘Pathetic’ and that playing them is ‘Weak.’

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/where-is-black-ops-royale-call-of-duty-warzone/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Ubisoft Really Wants You to Know That Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced is Not an RPG, as It Details Combat and HUD Changes
item.content:encoded:
Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced will feature updated combat — but Ubisoft really wants you to know that the game hasn't been changed to become an RPG.

In a lengthy post detailing the new combat changes and customizable HUD options it has added to the upcoming Black Flag remake, Ubisoft was again at pains to make clear that its updates and alterations had not radically changed the game's genre, to become more like recent entries.

"Although health and defense bars are present in default settings, this does not change the fact that Black Flag Resynced remains an action-adventure game," Ubisoft wrote. "The combat works similarly to the original game, with health and defense bars being optional. There are no levels, no gear scores and no progression gates."

The Complete Assassin's Creed Timeline

This is not the first time Ubisoft has sought to distance Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced from more recent games that have leaned into RPG mechanics to a greater and lesser extent, presumably in order to ensure the title launches to the widest possible audience — and doesn't give the suggestion it has been changed beyond recognition.

"Assassin's Creed: Black Flag Resynced matters deeply to us, and to you," game director Richard Knight said during the game's recent unveiling. "That's why we took the time to rebuild it with the care it deserves. This remains a solo adventure and character-driven experience. It is not an RPG. The focus stays on how you play, and how you explore the world."

Similar to Assassin's Creed Shadows, Ubisoft has made Black Flag Resynced's HUD fully customizable, allowing players to enable or disable most elements and pick from several preset defaults. For example, the game's "Simple" HUD offering will remove several elements, while still keeping some that will aid your combat and navigation. The "Minimal" HUD setting will pare the on-screen elements down further, displaying only essential information such as health and interaction prompts. Even these can be turned off too, should you wish.

Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Screenshots

By default, you'll see enemy health and defense bars — a choice Ubisoft has made to enable players to better understand Resynced's new enemy defense mechanic. (This is where timed parries, sustained or strong attacks break an enemy's defense, opening them up to a takedown.) But again, these elements can also be disabled.

After years of leaks, Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced is now just a few months from launch. The long-awaited remake is currently set to arrive on July 9 for PC, PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X/S, with a $200 Collector's Edition also available — including the same Edward Kenway statue that popped up on Vinted earlier this year.

Tom Phillips is IGN's News Editor. You can reach Tom at tom_phillips@ign.com or find him on Bluesky @tomphillipseg.bsky.social



https://www.ign.com/articles/ubisoft-really-wants-you-to-know-that-assassins-creed-black-flag-resynced-is-not-an-rpg-as-it-details-combat-and-hud-changes
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Star Wars: Galactic Racer launches October 6
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Available in standard, Deluxe, and Collector's editions.

Star Wars: Galactic Racer will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store on October 8, publisher Secret Mode and developer Fuse Games announced.

The game will be available in the following editions:

  • Standard Edition ($59.99 / £49.99 / €59.99)
    • A copy of the game
  • Deluxe Edition ($79.99 / £64.99 / €79.99)
    • A copy of the game
    • Steel case with sleeve (physical edition only)
    • Digital art book – Contains never-before-seen artwork of the characters, locations, and vehicles
    • Exclusive repulsorcrafts (x3)
      • Kor Sarun: Darc X landspeeder
      • Kor Sarun: Ciza T speeder bike,
      • Kor Sarun: Rak S skim speeder
    • Unique arcade events (x3)
    • Deluxe Livery Pack – Unique designs for all of your vehicles
    • Deluxe Player Banner Pack – New Insignia and Background for you to show your style in multiplayer modes
  • Collector’s Edition ($159.99 / £139.99 / €159.9)
    • Kor Sarun: Darc X landspeeder physical model
    • Galactic League champion Kestar Bool physical banner
    • Pilot patches (x2)
    • Physical art book
    • Steel case with custom slip cover
    • Deluxe Edition upgrade – Includes all the digital items from the Deluxe Edition

Pre-orders for all editions include a free bonus livery which can be used with landspeeders, speeder bikes, or skim speeders, with a unique color for the livery depending on the pre-order platform. All pre-orders also include a free Player Banner background for use in multiplayer modes.

  • ❮
  • ❯

Here is an overview of the game, via Secret Mode:

About

Star Wars: Galactic Racer is a runs-based, high-stakes reinvention of racing born in the lawless Outer Rim of the Star Wars galaxy.

The Empire’s grip on the galaxy has finally broken, and with its fall comes a new obsession: speed. As the New Republic struggles to rebuild, gambling, entertainment, and glory fuel a booming underground.

Out of this chaos, The Galactic League is born: an unsanctioned racing circuit where syndicates sponsor pilots, fortunes are won and lost in seconds, and only the bold survive.

No Force. No prophecy. Just skill, strategy, and the will to rise.

Key Features

  • Compete in High-stakes Races Where Every Run is Unique – Slam, shunt, and takedown rivals across varied circuits where no two runs play the same. The greater the risk, the greater the reward.
  • Survive a Dangerous Underground Sport – Join The Galactic League – an unsanctioned racing circuit born in the lawless Outer Rim. Become Shade, a lone racer chasing revenge and glory.
  • Build and Customize Your Craft  – Pilot different classes of repulsorcraft each with distinct physics and playstyles. Build a ride that showcases your style and gives you a competitive edge.
  • Battle Rivals in Multiple Game Modes – Ride solo through a story-driven campaign of shifting alliances or brave strategic player-versus-player grudge matches where reputation rides with you.
  • Climb the Ranks and Earn Rewards – Prove yourself through intense rivalries and escalating challenges. Every choice shapes your prospects as you build your status in The Galactic League.

Watch a new trailer below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/star-wars-galactic-racer-launches-october-6
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nacon Connect 2026 set for May 7
item.content:encoded:
Updates, announcements, and surprises abound.

Following a delay from its previously planned March 4 air date, Nacon Connect 2026 will now air on May 7 at 11:00 a.m. PT / 2:00 p.m. ET, Nacon announced.

The broadcast will feature “exciting updates on our biggest games, brand-new announcements, and exclusive surprises,” including The Mound, Edge of Memories, Endurance Motorsport Series, and Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish.

Watch a teaser trailer below.

Teaser Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/nacon-connect-2026-set-for-may-7
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dark fantasy roguelike deckbuilder Rogue Reigns announced for PS5, Xbox Series, Switch, PC, iOS, and Android
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Xbox Series

Due out in September.

Brazilian developer Venn Studios has announced Rogue Reigns, a dark fantasy roguelike deckbuilder. It will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch, PC via Steam, iOS, and Android in September. Demos will be playable at Gamescom LATAM 2026 from April 30 to May 3, and Gamescom 2026 from August 26 to 30. It will also be featured as part of the Steam Deckbuilders Fest from May 4 to 11.

“In most deckbuilders, you control one character and build one deck,” Venn Studios CEO Hugo Neri said in a press release. “In Rogue Reigns, three characters fight together—and the way they interact is the game. Your party composition is your build.”

Here is an overview of the game, via Venn Studios:

About

Rogue Reigns is a dark fantasy roguelike deckbuilder featuring hand-drawn characters set against procedurally generated 3D environments—a rarity within the deckbuilding genre—and an innovative party-based combat system.

Developed using Venn Studios’ proprietary Wildcard engine, Rogue Reigns represents a major technical and creative leap for the genre, blending hand-crafted character art and stylized 3D environments with the strategic depth of roguelike deckbuilding.

A New Evolution of Deckbuilding: “Your Party Is Your Build”

At its core, Rogue Reigns reimagines traditional deckbuilding by shifting the focus from a single character to a party of three heroes, each with their own deck, abilities, and turn order. The interplay between characters is where the game truly comes alive.

After every encounter, the party faces a choice: rewards are limited. One hero gets stronger. The others don’t. Over the course of a run, these small decisions accumulate—the hero you’ve been feeding grows powerful while the ones you’ve neglected fall behind. When the stalker catches up, someone falls.

Players will assemble a team from a roster of unique classes—including Warrior, Wizard, Rogue, Paladin, and Warlock—with each combination unlocking new synergies, strategies, and emergent gameplay possibilities. At launch, players can experiment with 10 distinct party compositions, ensuring no two runs feel the same.

A Dark Fantasy World Without Death

Set in fractured kingdoms where death itself has been abolished by a misused divine artifact, Rogue Reigns invites players into a decaying world filled with tragic rulers, broken societies, and slow-burning collapse.

This haunting setting is brought to life through a distinctive hand-crafted 3D art direction, combining bold, expressive character design with muted palettes and striking color accents—delivering a visual identity rarely seen in roguelike deckbuilders.

Built with the Community, for the Community

Rogue Reigns is the first title from Venn Studios, a Sao Paulo-based indie team that has spent years deeply embedded in the deckbuilding community.

Through their YouTube channel—now surpassing 108,000 subscribers—the team has built a reputation as passionate experts in the genre, creating content that explores mechanics, design philosophy, and emerging trends in roguelike deckbuilders.

This close relationship with players has directly shaped the game’s development. Rogue Reigns has been extensively refined through numerous playtests involving dedicated deckbuilder fans across the community, both at live events and through ongoing feedback loops. These sessions have played a key role in evolving the game’s core systems—particularly its party-based mechanics.

Venn Studios plans to continue expanding these playtests leading up to launch, opening them up to a broader audience and ensuring the community remains an active part of the development process.

Key Features

  • Party-Based Deckbuilding – Control three heroes simultaneously, each with their own deck and role.
  • Deep Synergy System – Character interactions create layered, emergent strategies.
  • Trust and Sacrifice – Rewards are scarce. Not every hero benefits equally. Favor one, and the others fall behind.
  • Hand-Drawn Characters, Procedural Worlds – Expressive character art meets generated 3D environments that react to combat.
  • The Stalker – Something hunts your party across the map. The world reacts before you see it.
  • Over 10 Party Compositions at Launch – High replayability with diverse builds and playstyles.
  • Proprietary Wildcard Engine – Built from the ground up for systemic gameplay and visual fidelity.
  • Striking 3D Visual Style – A rare aesthetic in the genre, blending stylization with depth.
  • Community-Driven Development – Shaped by player feedback across global playtests and events.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/dark-fantasy-roguelike-deckbuilder-rogue-reigns-announced-for-ps5-xbox-series-switch-pc-ios-and-android
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PopoloCrois creator Yosuke Tamori dies at age 74
item.content:encoded:
Esteemed creator passed away on April 10.

Yosuke Tamori, the manga artist and novelist who created PopoloCrois, passed away at the age 74 on April 10, according to an announcement on the Mangaka Association website.

Tamori first published the PopoloCrois manga in the magazine Dakkusu in 1978. In 1981, he began serialization of the manga series in Asashi Shougakusei Shimbun. In 1996, Sony Computer Entertainment (now Sony Interactive Entertainment) released a video game version of PopoloCrois for the original PlayStation, which spawned multiple sequels. It also received two anime adaptations in 1998 and 2003. Tamori also published various other manga and picture books in his career.

Thanks, Famitsu.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/popolocrois-creator-yosuke-tamori-dies-at-age-74
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Rebel Wolves Aimed for a 40-Hour RPG With The Blood of Dawnwalker, but Testers Are Burning up to 70 Hours
item.content:encoded:
We've known since the game's announcement that The Blood of Dawnwalker would not be quite as large as The Witcher III: Wild Hunt. This makes perfect sense, as Rebel Wolves is smaller than CD Projekt RED was when it made its masterpiece.

However, what exactly is the scope that players can expect in the full game? As reported by Gamereactor, Creative Director Mateusz Tomaszkiewicz revealed that The Blood of Dawnwalker turned out to be even bigger than the studio had originally planned. The goal was to target a 40-hour average playthrough, but now that all the content is in place and testing is being conducted, the average is between 50 and 70, presumably depending on how much time the player spends exploring Vale Sangora.

It's funny, because when we first started to work, we planned it for 40 hours. Right now, the average is around 50 hours, but it depends on which difficulty level you play, how picky you are, how much you explore, and how much you upgrade your gear, and so on. Lately, a few persons from our team finished the game… and there are big differences between them. It's like from 50 hours to around 70.

The low end is actually similar to The Witcher III: Wild Hunt, which averaged 51 hours and a half for a storyline-focused playthrough according to How Long to Beat.com. CD Projekt RED's game had much more side content, though, taking up to 170 hours for true completionists. Still, for the first game in what Rebel Wolves hopes will become a franchise, that's no slouch. It's certainly more than Obsidian's latest two action RPGs, Avowed and The Outer Worlds 2.

As covered yesterday, Tomaszkiewicz also revealed that a full evil playthrough is not only possible but also something he'd love to see from players.

The Blood of Dawnwalker launches September 3, 2026 for PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series S|X. You can find the detailed PC system requirements here.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/blood-of-dawnwalker-playtime-40-70-hours/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Heroes of Might and Magic: Olden Era hits early access today with the start of a narrative campaign, six factions, a map editor and multiplayer
item.content:encoded:
Heroes of Might and Magic: Olden Era is out in early access today, April 30th. It's taken just over a decade for Might & Magic Heroes VII to get a mailine follow-up that'll keep things heroic, mighty, and strategic, with Hooded Horse/Ubisoft-backed devs Unfrozen doing the honours.

HOMM: Olden Era's a strategic empire builder with turn-based fights and some RPG trimmings, pushing you to get off your fantasy bum and conquer both procedurally generated and hand-crafted maps to stave off the "impending doom" its campaign baddies threaten.

Both Julian and Edwin have given its early access version a go and reckon it's a strong reboot, but could stand to do a better job of helping you keep on top of what's happening as beasts, mages, and warriors flood your screen.

"Potentially a touch harshly I'm going to say its six legendary creatures out of ten for me," was the former's verdict. "I'm enjoying myself and starting to get a sense there is more under the surface than meets the eye, but I'm not yet sold on all the busywork of running back and forth across the map to collect piles of logs and recruit small gaggles of units."

"I give it seven griffons, which is easily enough to butcher a few upstart peasants, even peasants brandishing piglets," was Edwin's grading. "My main concern is that having to tend to my cities might drag more and more as I become more invested in my hero squads. Another argument for Olden Era is that there aren't many directly comparable games, even though it's been 10 years since the last proper HOMM – Songs of Conquest, I guess? Heroes of Might and Magic is very much its own style. I'd say Unfrozen are off to a good start with this comeback project."

Unfrozen's current plans are to have Olden Era early access run for a year, but this could change depending on the sort of feedback they get and how that influences the game's direction. As of right now, the goal's for the full release version to include "additional scenarios and map templates alongside improvements to the map editor and an additional Underground map layer", plus the acts of the campaign which follow the opening chapter that's available out of the early access gate.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/heroes-of-might-and-magic-olden-era-hits-early-access-today-with-the-start-of-a-narrative-campaign-six-factions-a-map-editor-and-multiplayer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New Golf Game Lets You Build Your Own Course And Then Play Your Horrible Ideas
item.content:encoded:
I stepped up to the tee and smacked my tiny white golf ball as hard as I could toward the red flag in the distance. But I forgot about the wind, and suddenly my ball veered left and landed in a sand trap. A big one. Right next to the green. What asshole built this course? Oh…right, I did.

Under Par Golf Architect is a new golf course management sim out now on PC and consoles. In Under Par, you’re tasked with creating profitable and popular golf courses. So you have to plan out each hole, making them properly difficult but still fun to play, while also managing customer needs like food and drink. And then you’ll need to hire some employees to help run snack stands and keep the place running while you’re busy expanding a hole or building a new restroom. If you’ve played any tycoon games, especially any from the late ’90s to mid-2000s, you’ll feel right at home with the level of nitty-gritty simulation you’ll need to worry about to make a thriving golf course. Sid Mier’s SimGolf fans, you are going to feel very much at home.

Under Par is clearly emulating that era of sim-management games. There are lots of colorful menus filled with rounded edges and comfy-looking icons. Clicking these will often result in a satisfying noise, too. But you’ll also notice wacky golfers walking about your course with exaggerated animations and big heads. Watching these dorks fail at a tricky hole is very fun. Watching them succeed, while less fun, is also nice and comes with a little pop of noise as well as a celebration animation. Of course, I was often too busy planning my next hole to watch the golfers have fun.

Making holes and hitting balls

Creating golf holes is the big focus of Under Par, and it thankfully nails this part of the game. You can dig in as much as you want on each course. Sure, you can slap down a few pieces of grass and a hole and call it a day. But golfers will give you a poor review, and that will hurt your overall rating. So you’re encouraged to get your hands dirty and place bushes, change elevation in certain spots, create traps, and make courses that will challenge without being completely impossible. Golfers hate that, too. So picky, these club-wielding bastards.

One of my favorite features of Under Par Golf Architect is the ability to play your own creation. At any point, with the simple click of a colorful button at the top of the screen, I was able to leave god mode and become a common golfer. I even walked around in a wacky manner, just like the little ants I usually lorded over. In this mode, you’re free to explore your course, interact with objects, and even play a few rounds on your own holes.

The golf simulation isn’t very deep, but it is just challenging enough that making a hole in one on a tricky course that you created feels great. Even better if you’ve watched a bunch of bozos fail at it earlier. Did I feel smug when I pulled it off? Yes. Yes, I did.

Under Par can be finicky at times. Placing fences, for example, drove me mad as they often erased sidewalks or grass. And I wish the financial part of Under Par was a bit more fleshed out and useful. Still, the game hooked me hard, and after what felt like just a few minutes, I had played for nearly two hours. In the middle of a workday. Whoops! If you are a fan of not-too-serious sim-management games like Two Point Museum or the aforementioned SimGolf, I think Under Par Golf Architect is worth a swing or two.



https://kotaku.com/new-golf-game-lets-you-build-your-own-course-and-then-play-your-horrible-ideas-2000691741
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Can You Romance Characters in Neverness to Everness? NTE Romance, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Neverness to Everness has a heap to explore and some eye-catching characters to meet, which understandably raises questions on whether there are romance options in the game.

Romance in gaming has become a popular pastime for players, allowing them to create a deeper bond with fan-favourites, as well as the potential to unlock exclusive missions, dialogue, and rewards.

If you’re wondering whether romance is available in Neverness to Everness, Insider Gaming has all the details you need.

Can You Romance Characters in Neverness to Everness?

Yes, romance options are available in Neverness to Everness, with a host of characters available to interact with and date.

Spending time with specific characters and handing them gifts boosts the character’s Bond Affinity System, unlocking additional rewards, dialogue moments, and encounters with them.

  • More Insider Gaming: How to Spawn Headless Rider Anomaly Boss in Neverness to Everness

Interactions for dates include going for a walk holding hands, driving around the city, riding a ferris wheel, and unlocking specific side stories with the character to complete.

Don’t expect any lewd scenes, however, as there are no such offerings in Neverness to Everness, and while romance is available, it remains suitable for a 13+ audience, with the main content advisory reasons being moderate violence and in-game purchases.

All Romance Characters in Neverness to Everness

All major characters in the launch roster are available to date, including fan favorites Mint, Aurelia, and Chiz.

Each character has likes and dislikes, with specific gifts boosting your Affinity Bond with them, so it’s worth looking into if you’re keen on rapidly progressing through the bond levels with a specific character.

  • More Insider Gaming: Where to Find The Witch House in Neverness to Everness

One guarantee to boost the bond of any character, however, is to hand them a Handwritten Letter, available in the Warp Exchange Circle Bounty.

The table below features all of the romance characters available in Neverness to Everness, and a few of their favourite gifts.

What’s your favourite feature in Neverness to Everness? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Neverness to Everness, find out how to play with friends in multiplayer, and check out all of the active codes to redeem.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/neverness-to-everness-romance-guide/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sony confirms it's added "one-time" DRM to digital PlayStation games requiring you to go online to avoid losing access, but doesn't comment on "CBOMB"
item.content:encoded:
Sony has issued a statement confirming the introduction of a new DRM online check for newly bought PlayStation games, reassuring the public that games can be bought and played as usual. This statement, however, does not address concerns around the "CBOMB" issue.

Released overnight to press following public confusion and investigation around the matter, the statement reads as follows:

"Players can continue to access and play their purchased games as usual. A one-time online check is required after purchase to confirm the game's license, after which no further check-ins are needed."

To summarise the issue, PlayStation owners discovered a 30-day license on newly bought PlayStation 4 games, as well as a 30-day countdown timer. When this timer reaches zero, access to the game is removed.

This sudden introduction of DRM was made without an official announcement by Sony, raising concerns that players would have to establish an internet connection every 30 days in order to retain access to purchased games. Shared screenshots of the PlayStation generative AI support (of debatable validity) did little to lessen confusion around the matter.

This, upon further investigation, turned out to not quite be the case. The 30-day validity timer was real on the PS4 and PS5, but if an online check was made 15 days after purchase, the offline limited license would transform into an indefinite offline license. It is speculated this is an attempt to curb a PSN refund exploit, as the deadline for refunding PlayStation games is 14 days. Sony, however, has not confirmed this outright.

There is, however, another element to the story still unaddressed by Sony's statement. The "CBOMB" issue, which refers to an inability to validate an online license once a PlayStation's CMOS battery has died, remains. As part of community testing, it was discovered that, with this new DRM in place, a console without a working CMOS battery would be unable to obtain this permanent offline license, locking players out from new games.

So why does this matter? In the future, when support for these consoles are no longer supported (like the PS3), access to newly bought games could be hampered. Sony's statement has not addressed this problem, though Sony has previously resolved similar "CBOMB" problems across its modern consoles.

So, to conclude, if you can establish an internet connection once following the purchase of a new PlayStation 4 or PlayStation 5 game, after that 14-day window, your license should remain valid indefinitely, and you won’t have any problem accessing your games in the future.

As for further updates on this CMOS issue, the future ramifications pending further statements by Sony remains unclear.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/sony-comment-playstation-30-day-drm
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: All Far Far West Codes & How to Redeem Them
item.content:encoded:
Far Far West is another breakout ‘friendslop’ game with a great concept and an even better look and feel. If you’re looking for monthly codes for Far Far West, you may be left feeling a bit disappointed for now.

The friendslop term has become an endearing one, with developers and publishers openly embracing the genre coinage; it’s a sign of acknowledgement that it works, and it’s spawned so many popular multiplayer games.

One of 2026’s newest additions is Far Far West, so let’s dive into codes information.

Far Far West devs have revealed that the game doesn’t have any monthly codes at present.

I said you might be left disappointed, and that’s because Far Far West doesn’t have monthly codes for you to pick up free items and goodies.

Confirmation comes from the official Far Far West Discord.

  • More Insider Gaming: All NTE: Neverness to Everness Codes & How to Redeem

Under the title’s FAQ, a question asks: ‘Can I ask for promo codes?’ The answer is a simple one for now:

‘There are no promo codes for the moment. Please do not request them in the general chat.’

This guide is up, though, in the event that Far Far West will get monthly codes. When it does, I will update this page, and Insider Gaming will bring you all the latest Far Far West codes and how to claim them.

The most logical assumption for Far Far West not having monthly codes right now is due to the title being an early access game.

This doesn’t apply to everyone and everything, but games being in early access means the team behind the game is more focused on delivering a solid, working game for customers who’ve committed to helping a new game at first with early access.

  • More Insider Gaming: NBA 2K26 Locker Codes: All Active Codes For April 2026

The FAQ doesn’t elaborate on the lack of monthly codes, but it could well be something we either see during early access at some point or closer to the 1.0 release.

Are you still enjoying Far Far West regardless of a lack of monthly codes? What makes it stand out from other friendslop games in recent years? Share your thoughts on the game with us through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out our updated monthly code lists for other games:

  • WWE 2K26 Locker Codes: All Active Codes For April 2026
  • All Escape from Tarkov Codes—Active Reward Codes and Free Items
  • Pokémon Champions Codes: All Active Mystery Gift Codes & How to Redeem Them

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/far-far-west-codes/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet is "a different side of Naughty Dog", says lead actress
item.content:encoded:
Naughty Dog's next game, Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet, is "a different side" of The Last of Us and Uncharted studio Naughty Dog.

That's according to lead actress Tati Gabrielle, who plays bounty hunter Jordan A Mun in the upcoming game.

Speaking with IGN at a recent press event for the Mortal Kombat 2 film, in which Gabrielle stars as assassin Jade, the actress said she was "super stoked" about Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet. "I am excited to play myself," Gabrielle said. "I think that it's such a different side of Naughty Dog that I think people are going to get really excited for."

Gabrielle added she believes Naughty Dog has "even challenged [itself] in ways of world building" on Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet, though she did not elaborate on this point any further.

Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet was first announced at the end of 2024, with a trailer featuring a retro futuristic energy that introduced Jordan A Mun. While we still don't know a huge amount about Naughty Dog's upcoming release beyond it being one expensive endeavour, game director Neil Druckmann revealed last March that Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophecy explores the topics of "faith and religion".

Last May, Gabrielle revealed she had been given mental "bootcamp-ing" by Druckmann ahead of her turn as Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet's bounty hunter, after actors in the studio's previous game The Last of Us Part 2 were met with online abuse. "[Druckmann] was like, 'Ignore it. No matter what, me and you, we're going to make something beautiful. We're going to make something that we're proud of'," Gabrielle said last year.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/intergalactic-the-heretic-prophet-naughty-dog-different-side
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
